title: Predicted Clinton Arrest and National Guard Reference
summary: The first Q drop claimed Hillary Clinton would be arrested and suggested National Guard mobilization. The prediction did not occur as stated, making it one of the earliest falsifiable Q claims.
raw_text: HRC extradition already in motion effective yesterday with several countries in case of cross border run. Passport approved to be flagged effective 10/30 @ 12:01am. Expect massive riots organized in defiance and others fleeing the US. US M's will conduct the operation while NG activated. Proof check: Locate a NG member and ask if activated for duty 10/30 across most major cities. Q

sources.label: Q Alerts Archive
sources.note: Archive of Q drops and timestamps

lines.text: HRC extradition already in motion effective yesterday
lines.fact: No public evidence or official documentation showed Hillary Clinton was being extradited in October 2017.
lines.theory: Q followers interpreted this as evidence of sealed indictments and a covert operation against Clinton.
lines.context: The statement appeared shortly before public awareness of QAnon rapidly expanded online.
lines.questions: What official extradition request or court filing would verify this claim? Was any evidence produced later?
lines.Deltas: 2018-2025: No verified arrest or extradition of Hillary Clinton occurred on matching yearly deltas.

lines.fact: National Guard units regularly conduct exercises and emergency readiness operations, but no evidence confirmed a nationwide activation tied to Clinton.
lines.theory: Supporters believed military preparations were connected to anticipated unrest and mass arrests.
lines.context: NG is commonly interpreted as National Guard in early Q drops.
lines.questions: Were there any unusual National Guard mobilizations documented for October 30, 2017?
